title: Windy Starkiller

{{Quote|Windy's nothing but a follower…|[[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]]|<ref name="SWradio">[[Star Wars (radio)|''Star Wars'' radio drama]]</ref>}}
'''Windy Starkiller''', otherwise known as '''Windom Marstrap''', was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], and a childhood friend of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] when he lived on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. He often spent time with Luke at [[Tosche Station/Legends|Tosche Station]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
[[File:AtTheVaporator-SWIANH.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Windy mocks his friend Luke Skywalker about his dream to join the Imperial Academy.]]
Windy Starkiller was a male [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="ANH" /> born on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] around [[19 BBY/Legends|the same time]] as his future friend [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]<ref name="birth" /> from a [[Windy Starkiller's father|couple of]] [[Windy Starkiller's mother|local inhabitants]].<ref name="lswalkabout" />

Windy was usually seen in the company of his friend [[Deacon|Deak]], who continually followed him around [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]]. Another [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] acquaintance, [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]], once characterized Windy as "a follower" and called Deak "a follower of a follower."<ref name="SWradio" />

[[File:Windy_Galaxy_2.jpg|thumb|180px|Windy in 0 BBY]]
Windy's parents would sometimes visit [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke's]] [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|aunt]] and [[Owen Lars/Legends|uncle]] at the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]]. When they were thirteen, Luke and Windy became lost in the desert and found themselves face to face with a [[Krayt dragon/Legends|Krayt dragon]]. They were rescued by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], whom they only knew as hermit Ben Kenobi. When Windy and Luke were brought back safely to the Lars homestead by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Owen angrily chased Kenobi away.<ref name="adventurebeggars">''[[Adventure in Beggar's Canyon]]''</ref><ref name="lswalkabout">{{StoryCite|story=Luke Skywalker's Walkabout|book=Dark Horse Presents Annual 1999: DHP Jr.}}</ref>
{{Talesstart}}
Windy and Deak were present when Luke was challenged to a speeder race by a local braggart, [[Gorm Maldorf]], in [[The Cage]]. He warned Luke that Maldorf was dangerous, but he accepted the challenge. On the morning of the race, Gorm was found [[Death/Legends|dead]], strangled, in The Cage. After Windy and Deak helped him make his escape, Luke investigated the death and deduced that Gorm had been accidentally killed while trying to sabotage the race the night before.<ref name="lsdetective">{{Tales|20|Luke Skywalker: Detective}}</ref>
{{Talesend}}
[[File:Windyadult.jpg|thumb|left|Windy in his late teens]]
By their late teens, the friends were part of a group of youths that included Biggs Darklighter, [[Janek Sunber/Legends|Janek "Tank" Sunber]], [[Laze Loneozner/Legends|Laze "Fixer" Loneozner]], and his girlfriend [[Camie Loneozner|Camie]]. The teens spent time hanging around at [[Tosche Station/Legends|Tosche Station]] in Anchorhead, and raced their [[T-16 skyhopper/Legends|skyhoppers]] for fun.<ref name="SWradio" /><ref name="RS3">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''</ref>

When Biggs was accepted into the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]], the friends and other locals threw him a party that was broken up by attacking [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Sand people]].<ref name="mc17">[[Star Wars (1977) 17|''Star Wars'' (1977) 17]]</ref>

Windy would often serve as a foil to Luke's dreams of leaving Tatooine to join the Academy. Once, when Windy found Luke replaying a Space Academy Recruitment tape for a countless time, he told his friend that he should grow up and be content with the life of a [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmer]].<ref name="SWJ">''[[Star Wars Journal: The Fight for Justice]]''</ref>

===Galactic Civil War===
[[File:Deak_and_Windy_-_Empire_8.jpg|200px|thumb|Deak and Windy at Tosche Station]]
Sometime later, just before the [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']] was captured by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] above Tatooine, Luke challenged Fixer to a dangerous skyhopper race through [[Beggar's Canyon/Legends|Beggar's Canyon]] and through the eye of the [[Stone Needle/Legends|Stone Needle]]. Windy flew along with Luke during the race, squealing with fear the whole time. He was frightened by Skywalker's reckless flying and called him "crazy."<ref name="SWradio" /><ref name="SWJ" />

Windy saw Luke on the morning Biggs confided in Luke about his plans to join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Windy, along with the rest of his friends, told Luke that he did not believe he had actually seen a [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|firefight]] in the skies above Tatooine. Shortly after, Luke left and eventually participated in the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="SWradio" /><ref name="SWJ" /> Months later Windy saw Luke again when the Rebel returned briefly to Tatooine while he was trying to hide from [[X-7]].<ref name="RF:R">''[[Rebel Force: Renegade]]''</ref>

More than a year after the Battle of Yavin, Windy could still be found in Anchorhead. While there, he asked a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] to find a wanted murderer named [[Blix Lanister]], who had fled town.<ref name="SWG">{{GalaxiesAED}}</ref>

{{Gamemechanics}}
[[File:WindomStarkiller.jpg|thumb|left|Windy around [[1 ABY/Legends|1.5 ABY]]]]
The spacer agreed to carry out the task, and found Blix shortly thereafter. Blix was brought back to Windy, who paid the spacer 30 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] in thanks. He even said the spacer should consider a career as a security officer. Yet the situation with Blix did not end there. Windy also wanted the spacer to find one of Blix's gang members, a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] named [[Gleezwuq]]. Gleezwuq was the only member of Blix's gang whose whereabouts Windy did not know. He was sure that Gleezwuq would have evidence which would implicate Blix in his murder of [[Alita Sweetwater]].<ref name="SWG" />

The spacer managed to find the Rodian gang member, and killed him. On Gleezwuq's person was the same [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] that Blix used in his murder of Alita. The spacer brought the blaster back to an impressed Windy, who was now certain that Blix could be brought to justice. The spacer was once again awarded another 30 credits. However, shortly after the evidence was brought to Windy, Blix's cohorts came into town and helped Blix escape Anchorhead. Windy tasked the spacer with one final task, which was to find Blix and his cronies. Windy did not believe that Blix would let himself be brought back alive again. He told the spacer to do what was necessary. Ultimately, the spacer found Blix, and killed him and the other thugs. Following the successful operation, Windy awarded the spacer 100 credits. Sometime following this event, Windy left Anchorhead.<ref name="SWG" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Tosche_Station_-_TMOSW.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A glimpse of Windy in the back of Tosche Station, with Biggs and Deak]]
Windy is barely glimpsed in the background of the Tosche Station scene [[Content cut from Star Wars#Episode IV - A New Hope|cut]] from [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']], playing a game with Biggs and Deak. It's not obvious which of the two men is Windy and which is Deak. The scene is included in the Blu-Ray release of the film. His character is expanded upon in the [[Star Wars (radio)|''Star Wars'' radio drama]], where he gets several lines of dialogue.<ref name="SWradio" />

Windy appears briefly in a training level of the video game ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'', voiced by [[Andrew Chaikin]].<ref name="RS3" />

===Continuity===
In the original version of ''[[Luke Skywalker's Walkabout]]'', Windy is referred to as both Windom Starkiller and Windy Marstrap. One can assume that Windy is short for Windom. [[Leland Chee]] has stated that his last name is Marstrap in the [[Holocron continuity database]].<ref name="Holocron">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1335#16585930|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2007-05-12|time=4:53 PM|quote=Windy's last name is Marstrap. That's about all I've got.}}</ref> However, in the colored reprint for ''[[Star Wars: Luke Skywalker, Last Hope for the Galaxy]]'', Windy's last name has been corrected, and he is named Starkiller both times. Confusing things further, an action figure released by [[Hasbro]] in 2010 gave Windy's friend Camie the last name "Marstrap."

''[[Adventure in Beggar's Canyon]]'' and ''[[Luke Skywalker's Walkabout]]'' were both suggested by a single line of dialogue from Luke in the ''Star Wars'' radio drama. The two stories are variations on the same premise.<ref name="adventurebeggars" /><ref name="lswalkabout" />

In "[[Luke Skywalker: Detective]]," the artist makes Windy and Deak virtually identical, and it's never identified which character is which.<ref name="lsdetective" />

[[File:FemaleWindy.jpg|250px|thumb|Windy depicted as a girl instead of as a boy in ''[[Luke's Fate]]'', unlike all of his other appearances.]]
In the [[Wikipedia:MMORPG|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', Windy was named Windom Starkiller and could be found as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|Non-Player Character]] (NPC) in a tent in Anchorhead. Players who performed a series of [[:w:c:Quest|quests]] for Windy had to escort Blix Lanister to Anchorhead, kill Gleezwuq outside [[City/Legends|town]], and then kill Lanister when he escaped from Anchorhead. The series of quests involved implicating Blix Lanister in the murder of Alita Sweetwater. During the second quest, Windom Starkiller would ask the player to call him "Windy." In the [[Star Wars video games|game]], Windy had [[Color/Legends|blond]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] with brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. For unknown reasons, Windy was removed from ''Star Wars Galaxies'' less than a year after its release, during [[:w:c:swg:Publish 6 Notes:11 February 04|Publish 6]] on [[February 11]], [[2004]]. Although the player could choose not to do Windy's quests, this article assumes 100% game completion.<ref name="SWG" />

In the book ''[[Luke's Fate]]'' by [[Jim Thomas]], Windy is depicted as a young girl, in contrast with his usual masculine representation and pronouns.<ref name="Luke's Fate">''[[Luke's Fate]]''</ref>

"Windy" was the name of a character in early versions of the ''Star Wars'' script. The first iteration of Windy was as "[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windy]]," a Jedi, in the ''[[Journal of the Whills, Part I]]''. In the [[The Star Wars rough draft|rough draft]], Windy becomes the son of [[King/Legends|King]] [[Kayos]] and brother of Biggs and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]]. In the [[Adventures of the Starkiller, Episode I: The Star Wars|second draft]], Windy, with Biggs, his twin, is a younger brother of Luke Starkiller. They are definitively excised from Luke's family tree in the [[The Star Wars: From The Adventures of Luke Starkiller|third draft]], where they first become Luke's friends at the power station.<ref name="The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film">''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]''</ref>
